100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 5, 1926, the British government announced new measures to address the rising tension in India, where calls for independence were intensifying. In the United States, the Supreme Court upheld the prohibition laws, reinforcing the ongoing nationwide ban on alcohol. Meanwhile, in Europe, a devastating earthquake struck the region of Calabria in Italy, resulting in significant casualties and damage.
